topics.summary: Current Grade 9 Math work is focused on Cycle 3. Based on recent Google Classroom posts and your note, the next test range covers lessons 3.01 through the current lesson.
formulas: Use multiple representations: words, tables, graphs, and equations.
Compare linear and non-linear patterns by how they change across representations.
solvingSteps: Review 3.01 to the current lesson in order.
Identify the main idea of each lesson before practising questions.
Switch between graph, table, equation, and word-problem forms.
Check which topics still feel weak before the next test.
commonMistakes: Treating different representations as separate skills instead of connected ideas.
Mixing up linear and non-linear patterns.
Rushing through word problems without identifying the relationship first.
practiceEasy: Review boxplots and explain what the median and spread show.
Plot and read points on the coordinate plane.
practiceMedium: Compare a table and graph to decide whether the pattern is linear or non-linear.
Translate a short word problem into a table or equation.
practiceHard: Complete one mixed review set using topics from 3.01 through the current lesson.
Solve an equation and explain how it connects to a pattern or representation.
